title: Broken/dead links found in README
author: tracedynamics
body:
Hi! I was scanning the README for broken links and found these:

1. **http://thinkster.io** - DNS resolution failed (domain no longer resolves, listed in JavaScript section)
2. **http://sahatyalkabov.com/create-a-character-voting-app-using-react-nodejs-mongodb-and-socketio** - 404 (React/Node.js tutorial)
3. **http://dev.stephendiehl.com/fun/** - Connection timed out (Haskell tutorial)

Would you be open to a PR that replaces these with working alternatives? Happy to submit one.

title: 🔗 Link rot report
author: github-actions[bot]
body:
# 🔗 Link rot report

_Regenerated weekly by `.github/workflows/link-rot.yml`. Please open a pull request to fix an entry -- don't reply here with fixes, this issue body is fully rewritten on every run._

🤖 An automated fix is open: https://github.com/practical-tutorials/project-based-learning/pull/903 -- it only covers confirmed-dead removals and moved-URL updates below; review and merge it, or edit it further before merging. Suspect links always need a human look, and aren't included.

title: Broken Link in Go for Create a Real Time Chat App with Golang, Angular 2, and WebSocket
author: Shadowwfall
body: # Broken link in "Go" ## Expected Behavior The [Create a Real Time Chat App with Golang, Angular 2, and WebSocket](https://www.thepolyglotdeveloper.com/2016/12/create-real-time-chat-app-golang-angular-2-websockets/) link should take the user to the project site ## Current Behavior The link shows Page not found ## Possible Solution Refresh the link ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Click on the repo 2. In README.md scroll down to Go or click Go in TAble of Contents 3. Click the first project link Create a Real Time Chat App with Golang, Angular 2, and WebSocket ## Context This issue has affected me deeply. I would like to start my journey in GO by this project but it seems to not work.

title: [Suggestion] Add Next.js 14/15 (App Router) tutorials to the JS/TS section
author: webdevpraveen
body:
Hi team!

Since the React ecosystem has heavily shifted towards the Next.js App Router, it would be awesome to see some updated full-stack project tutorials using Next.js 14/15 in the JavaScript/TypeScript section. 

Currently, many tutorials focus on older React versions. Let me know if you're open to this, and I can hunt down some high-quality, free tutorials and open a PR!

title: Broken Link: Erlang -> "Making a Chat App with Erlang, Rebar, Cowboy and Bullet" leads to 404 error
author: rimsha-shoukat
body:
## Broken Link

###  Erlang Section

**Tutorial Name:** Making a Chat App with Erlang, Rebar, Cowboy and Bullet
**Location:** Erlang section

---
### **Current Behavior:**
The link returns a **404 Not Found** error.
```
404 Not Found
nginx/1.18.0 (Ubuntu)
```
The website appears to be permanently down.

---
### **Steps to Reproduce:**
1. Go to `README.md`
2. Navigate to the **Erlang** section
3. Click on **"Making a Chat App with Erlang, Rebar, Cowboy and Bullet"**
4. Observe the error

---
### Possible Solution
Remove the broken link or replace them with similar working alternatives.

title: Broken link: Build a Google+ clone with Django and AngularJS (Angular 1.x)
author: Shruti0460
body:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
The tutorial link in the Angular section is broken and returns a 502 Bad Gateway error.
**Tutorial Name:** Build a Google+ clone with Django and AngularJS (Angular 1.x)
**Broken Link:** https://thinkster.io/django-angularjs-tutorial
**Location in README:**  Angular section
### Expected Behavior
The link should direct users to a working tutorial or be removed from the list.


### Current Behavior
When clicking on the tutorial link, the browser shows:
- **502 Bad Gateway ERROR**
- Message: "The request could not be satisfied"
- The website appears to be permanently down

### Possible Solution
Remove the broken link or replace it with a similar working tutorial.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Go to the README.md file
2. Navigate to JavaScript > Angular section
3. Click on "Build a Google+ clone with Django and AngularJS (Angular 1.x)" link
4. Observe the 502 error
